characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Always Someone Better: First it was Angela, then briefly Holly until she showed her true colours. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Mac, before they get to know each other. Also has this with Guy later on. Born Unlucky: Her first day at the hospital sums this up nicely. She is locked out of her brother's house and has to sleep in her car, Joanna pulls her hair (to "see how well she deals with stress"), everyone keeps asking her why she smells funny, "Have you been to a festival?", "What's that smell? Is that...is that mini-cabs?" Sue gropes her arse, she hears Angela telling Martin to do horrible things to "the new girl" (because Mac told her to try being jealous) Guy persuades her to come stay at his flat and so everyone assumes she slept with him when she didn't. And then there's what happens to her during the rest of the series... Boyish Short Hair: Lampshaded by a few characters, who tell her she looks a bit like a man. Brainy Brunette: Downplayed as most of the main cast are doctors, but she's a competent surgeon and forges a successful career abroad in the sequel series, even inventing something called the "Todd clamp".

characters.name: Dr. Caroline Todd
characters.description: Always Someone Better: First it was Angela, then briefly Holly until she showed her true colours. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Mac, before they get to know each other. Also has this with Guy later on. Born Unlucky: Her first day at the hospital sums this up nicely. She is locked out of her brother's house and has to sleep in her car, Joanna pulls her hair (to "see how well she deals with stress"), everyone keeps asking her why she smells funny, "Have you been to a festival?", "What's that smell? Is that...is that mini-cabs?" Sue gropes her arse, she hears Angela telling Martin to do horrible things to "the new girl" (because Mac told her to try being jealous) Guy persuades her to come stay at his flat and so everyone assumes she slept with him when she didn't. And then there's what happens to her during the rest of the series... Boyish Short Hair: Lampshaded by a few characters, who tell her she looks a bit like a man. Brainy Brunette: Downplayed as most of the main cast are doctors, but she's a competent surgeon and forges a successful career abroad in the sequel series, even inventing something called the "Todd clamp".

characters.name: Dr. "Mac" Macartney
characters.description: The Ace: A very competent surgeon, handsome, nice, popular with almost everyone in the hospital and owns a very cool motorbike. Animal Motifs: He's sometimes compared to a lion because of his hair. Badass Biker: Has a motorbike, which he keeps in one of the lifts in the hospital. He also likes to drive around on it naked. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Caroline, at least when they first meet. Beware the Nice Ones:He's usually laid-back about all the bullying in the hospital, but people messing too much with Martin is hisBerserk Button, as Guy found out the hard way.He's noticeably annoyed when Guy starts hitting on a very drunk Caroline,although Guy doesn't take it further than kissing.He stabs guy in the arm when Guy refuses to stop poking his sore spot concerning Holly.

characters.name: Dr. Guillaume "Guy" Secretan
characters.description: All Men Are Perverts: Verging on a case ofExtreme Libido. Pretty much everything he says and does is with the end goal of getting laid. Ambiguously Bi: We only ever see him pursue women, but he says he knows how big Mac's penis is because he watches him in the shower. He also thought he had slept with both Mac and Caroline at some point. Animal Motifs: Several people reference the fact he looks like a donkey. (Specifically the one fromShrek.) Belligerent Sexual Tension:With Caroline, as he often goes out of his way to irritate her, but they do obviously have chemistry.Somewhat zigzagged with Sue White - he's terrified of her but he also seems weirdly aroused by it, too. With Caroline, as he often goes out of his way to irritate her, but they do obviously have chemistry.

characters.name: Dr. Alan Statham
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: Joanna doesn't seem to like him much even when they're together, and he later plays this role to Cordelia. Ax-Crazy: He psychoticallybeat a dwarf to death with a stuffed heron. Bad Liar: Most of the time he can't even get to the actual lying stage, and ends up blurting out the truth instead. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Boyce, of all people. Alan insists he's straight, but there are a few hints he may actually find Boyce attractive. Butt-Monkey: He is constantly mocked by his own students and his girlfriend insists on keeping their relationship a secret because she's embarrassed to be seen with him. He also gets himself into extremely embarrassing situations because of his arrogance and complete lack of social skills.

characters.name: Dr. Angela Hunter
characters.description: The Ace: Seems to be this in the first episode, but her more unsavory personality quirks come to light as the show goes on. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Often subtly undermining towards Caroline and likes being the centre of attention. Her bitchiness gets turned up to eleven after she becomes the face of a TV documentary set at the hospital. Interestingly, in a deleted scene with Sue White, Angela even lampshades it, saying she is ambitious, driven and will doanythingto get ahead. The "niceness" is all a front. It's hard to tell if she meant it or if she was trying to big herself up to Sue, however. Control Freak: Sheironsher pants, and when Caroline throws a house party, she follows her boyfriend Liam around, pestering him to "line his stomach", even when he insists he's not hungry. Covert Pervert: Acts all perky and squeaky-clean, but also claims she gets off on "sticking [her] hand up women and delivering babies" and she finds jealousy "exhilarating! In-in a dirty sort of way..." Elegant Classical Musician: She's beautiful and plays aHarp of Femininityto Grade 7, while her moretomboyishhousemate Caroline struggles to get a sound out of her flute:Dr. Caroline Todd:"Isn't it a shame you never made Grade 8!"

characters.name: Dr. Holly Hawkes
characters.description: Baby Trap: Shows up in series 2 with a young son in tow,claiming Mac is the dad. He isn't. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: She claims she wants to be friends with Caroline and is very touchy-feely with her, but immediately calls her "that weirdo Todd" when Mac pretends to choose her over Caroline. The Ghost: In Season 1 she's mentioned but never seen. She then appears in season 2. Implausible Deniability: Ironically a trait she shares with Caroline. But she keeps trying to maintain thatMackenzie is Mac's son, even when he produces not only orange hair-dye (she claims it's for a friend who is"phobic, for some reason, about buying hair-dye"), but alsoa DNA test. Mama's Baby, Papa's Maybe:Tells Mac that her son Mackenzie is his child, until Sue discovers evidence to the contrary and he secretly gets a DNA test done.

characters.name: Dr. Martin Dear
characters.description: Annoying Younger Sibling:To Guy. Bumbling Sidekick: Often plays this role to the more capable Mac or Guy. Butt-Monkey: He's constantly bullied by Guy (among others) and almost nothing goes right for him. Casanova Wannabe: Martin is the polar opposite of Guy in every way, a relatively unpopular, socially awkward and rather dumb individual who spends the series in totally unrequited love with Caroline. Rachel's pet nickname for him is "Martin Dear, isn't he queer!"Mind you, he does end up in a relationship with Karen Ball, but not many people would count that. Especially as he instantly regrets it when Karen turns out to be more demanding sexually than he can cope with.He accidentally becomes a pimp to "Double-D" Suzie as well. Mind you, he does end up in a relationship with Karen Ball, but not many people would count that. Especially as he instantly regrets it when Karen turns out to be more demanding sexually than he can cope with.

characters.name: Boyce
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: Has a girlfriend but ends up in bed with Alan and offers Martin a handjob at one point. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Kim, though more so on her side. Also has shades of this with Alan. Big Damn Kiss: He gets a pretty damn big one with Alan, in the series 2 finale. Casual Kink: In the finale, he, Alan and Joanna make out with each other. He proposes that they all go to a motel together. Cool Kid-and-Loser Friendship: With Martin, who Boyce gets along very well with despite how awkward and nerdy Martin is.

characters.name: Sue White
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: She's obsessed with Mac but also kisses Caroline several times and makes some sexually charged comments about women. Ax-Crazy: Offers tokillDr Mancoo so Mac can take over his job as consultant. We later see her cutting the brakes on his car. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Guy. She buggered him senseless once and has had a thing for him ever since, but he's just sort of terrified of her. She does manage to give him a hard-on just by talking to him, a few times over, and he asks her for another shag at least once - but all he gets is a date after the Slave Auction, and she makes him put on a blonde wig and pretend to be Mac. Comedic Sociopathy: Her hobbies including stalking people, molesting people, threatening to put SPELLS on people and shooting at people with a bow and arrow. She also gives birth to a lion cub, which raises a whole lot of questions that don't need to be answered. Crazy Cat Lady: She inexplicably has a few scenes in Season 1 riding a camel. She often barks like a dog and once says she will only answer Alan's questions "in the language of a crow".

characters.name: Joanna Clore
characters.description: Abusive Parents: She is clearly the worst mother in the universe,but it never stops Martin from declaring his undying love for her.Her interactions with Guy after Season One are horrendously awkward, but she does at least ask (if incredibly grudgingly) after his wellbeing. All Women Are Lustful: It says something that she was desperate enough to start a sexual relationship with Alan Statham, and there's her relentless pursuit of Lyndon andactually getting annoyed when builders don't wolf-whistle at her. Bad Boss: She physically and verbally abuses not just her regular HR underlings, but also various doctors and surgeons around the building. Berserk Button: If you want an easy life, don't make references to her age. Birthday Hater: Because it just means she's getting older. However, she seemed disappointed when it turned out the HR staff were not throwing her a surprise party.Not that you could blame them.

characters.name: Karen Ball
characters.description: A-Cup Angst: At one point she furiously yells at the other girls for walking all over her, "Just because I haven't got any breasts!" before sitting down and resuming work like nothing happened. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: She spends the first series as a bullied, self-loathing, friendlessButt-Monkey. By the time the Special rolls around, she is a flashy, arrogant, domineering bitch who eclipses Rachel in social status and positively terrifies the rest. Granted, in her last appearance before the special, she got knocked out of a window and nobody even noticed. Butt-Monkey: Getting her hair stuck in the printer, a Turkish man who barely speaks any English manages to string enough together to compare her to Brian May fromQueen (Band), she gets knocked out of a window and not evenMartinparticularly wants to go out with her. Curly Hair Is Ugly: As you might expect from a series set in the mid-2000s, a few characters make fun of her hair. Distaff Counterpart: To Martin, in both looks and personality.

characters.name: Harriet Schulenburg
characters.description: Bad Liar: She goes into involuntary spasms of blinking and facial twitching whenever she lies. Beware the Nice Ones: She can be pretty assertive when pushed too far. Cant Get Away With Nothing: Even though her husband has been cheating on her for some time, when Harriet starts an affair with Lyndon she can't bring herself to continue because she doesn't want to hurt her children, then Ian finds out about it anyway and kicks Harriet out. Comically Missing the Point: Often. Covert Pervert:She seems sexually frustrated, as her husband "hasn't slept with her since Oscar was born" and almost cries when she gets a "twinge down there."She also asks Lyndon to call her "Giselle" when they have sex.

characters.name: Kim Alabaster
characters.description: Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Boyce, even after they have sex at Caroline's party. Eye Scream: Gets sperm in her eye in episode one and has to wear an eye patch. Serious Business: Minestrone isnota flavour. Troll: To Joanna, but she really does deserve it.

characters.name: "Naughty" Rachel
characters.description: All There in the Manual: No one ever actually calls her "Naughty Rachel", that's just how she's referred to in the credits. Big-Breast Pride: Very proud of her huge boobs. She even weighs them in one episode. Deadpan Snarker: Mainly towards Karen and Martin. Distaff Counterpart: SheReally Gets Around, like Guy. The Ditz: She has her moments, like not realising she could pull up the handles on her suitcases.

characters.name: Lyndon Jones
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: Lyndon finally snaps after Joanna buys him in the Slave Auction and gives her a"The Reason You Suck" Speech, calling her "tedious, disturbing and desperate". Black and Nerdy: He's the IT guy, though he doesn't really seem like a stereotypical nerd. Chick Magnet: a handsome, sweet-natured IT consultant who is regularly called out to perform mundane, bullshit tasks by regular nurses and clerks who find themselves besotted with him. Deadpan Snarker: Usually toward either Guy or Alan, both of whom are jealous of his effortless ability to attract women. Heterosexual Life-Partners: Implied this is the direction his friendship with Jake might go.

characters.name: Jake Leaf
characters.description: Granola Girl: A male example - deals in alternative therapies like acupuncture and chiropractics. Heterosexual Life-Partners: His last scene implies he and Lyndon will develop into this. Long Hair Is Feminine: It's only about shoulder-length, but it doesn't stop Joanna referring to him as "Alice." Romantic False Lead: Works well as this because he's likable enough in his own right, it's just that Caroline and Mac have a strong connection despite Holly's interference. He comes off a bit like aThird-Option Love Interestfor Caroline (compared to Guy and Mac), but things don't work out. Suddenly Shouting: When Caroline is breaking up with him.

characters.name: Lady Emily "Emmy" Lewis-Westbrooke
characters.description: Dumb Blonde: While very sweet, she's not exactly the brightest button in the box. Named Like My Name: There's another Emily Lewis-Westbrooke, who is merely pretending to be a physiotherapist and is actually a con artist. Caroline is rather dismayed to learn she's got the wrong Emily Lewis-Westbrooke. Spoiled Sweet: Caroline and Guy make fun of her behind her back for being too posh. She even has a title. Nonetheless, she's infallibly lovely to everyone. Romantic False Lead: Briefly dates Mac in Season One, just to make things more frustrating for Caroline, but she clearly doesn't really "get" Mac's sense of humour.

characters.name: Cordelia Denby
characters.description: Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: She's blonde and very kind and patient. Friend to All Children: She's shown reading Bible stories aloud to the hospital's young patients (and Dr Statham). Sexy Priest: Alan certainly thinks so.
